description:
Phase 1 of fz-ul4.23. Wire up the fixture matrix so the rest of the epic has a harness to be judged by.

## Deliverables

- A `fixture_matrix` cargo test target that walks fixtures/, parses each .fz header (`# purpose:` and `# paths:`), runs each fixture through each listed path, compares stdout against `<name>.expected` (empty if absent), and asserts exit 0.
- Sidecar protocol:
  - `<name>.fz` — the program plus header comments.
  - `<name>.expected` — canonical stdout. Trailing newline normalized. Absent = empty stdout expected.
  - `<name>.output` — written on failure for diffing. Gitignored.
- `--bless` flag (env var or test arg) that overwrites `.expected` from current output.
- `fixtures/index.md` regenerated from headers; CI fails if stale.
- Header retrofit on every existing fixture: `# purpose:` line, `# paths: jit` (until interp/aot land).
- Add fixtures/ to .gitignore for `.output` only.

## Initial path scope

`paths: jit` is the only legal value for Phase 1 — interp and aot will be added as Phases 5/6 land. The runner must accept this and not assume parity yet.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test fixture_matrix` is green on existing fixtures.
- `fixtures/index.md` exists and matches headers.
- Touching a fixture's output and re-running with `--bless` updates the `.expected`.
title: Fixture runner with sidecar .expected + index.md regen
